rulings.Case Title: ERIC HUANG VS. VORRIECE A. COOPER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And For Award Of Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 3, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF ERIC HUANG Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And For Award Of Attorneys Fees is DENIED. No order retaining jurisdiction on file prior to dismissal. Sayta v. Chu (2017) 17 Cal.App.5th 960. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN RYAN VS. 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To The Foruth Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 3, 2021 line 2. Defendant 1001 California Street Condominium Homeowners Association's Demurrer To The Fourth Amended Complaint (PART ONE OF TWO) The Court makes the following preliminary rulings: (1) all references to events occurring after the original due date for the opposition to the demurrer are stricken from Plaintiff's opposition. The Court expressly instructed Plaintiff to "address solely the issues raised in the demurrers as if filed at the time when opposition was originally due;" (2) Judge Wong's order to continue trial was executed before this Court ruled on Defendant's MSA; there is no indication anywhere on the record that judge Wong at the time contemplated that this action will not be at issue prior to the new trial date; that there will be additional iterations on the complaint; or precluded any of the defendants from responding to the new versions of the complaint as statutorily allowed. On August 26, 2020 this Court granted Defendants motion for judgment on the pleadings (MSA was treated as the motion for judgment on the pleadings) with leave to amend based on the arguments advanced in Plaintiff's opposition to the MSA. Those arguments appeared to suggest that Plaintiff was capable of making factual allegations supporting the claims for breach of contract and violation of Davis-Sterling Act. The Court specifically instructed Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of these causes of action. As to the cause of action for breach of contract/governing documents for reasons known only to Plaintiff, Plaintiff added conclusory statements that section 6.01 of CC&Rs was breached (compare to argument and purported facts listed in opposition to MSA 11-12 and note that Section 6.01 is two pages long consisting of subsections (a)-(d)). Plaintiff did not quote this provision and did not attach the CC&Rs. Review of Plaintiff's opposition to the demurrer (Opposition 4:11-13) suggests that Plaintiff believes that assertions in opposition to the MSA form the basis of or are incorporated into the 4th Amended Complaint. Plaintiff does not cite any authority for this novel proposition. In opposition to the demurrer Plaintiff also advances an unsupported argument that legal issues cannot be properly addressed on a demurrer and have to be decided by a trier of fact (Opposition 11:17-18).

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN RYAN VS. 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant 1001 California Street'S Demurrer To The Foruth Amended Complaint (Part Of 2 0f 2 For Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 3, 2021 line 2. Defendant 1001 California Street Condominium Homeowners Demurrer To The Foruth Amended Complaint (Part Of 2 0f 2 For Tentative Ruling) Existence of a contractual obligation and breach of that obligation are basic factual allegations required to state a cause of action for breach of contract. Assuming that the Court takes judicial notice of the CC&Rs (there appears to be no dispute as to the content of this document attached to multiple filings in this case by both sides), the Court will now determine (as it did on its ruling on motion for Judgment on the Pleadings) if the 4th Amended Complaint may be amended to properly state facts supporting a breach of contract. While the Court must accept for the purposes of a demurrer Plaintiff's reasonable interpretation of the contract (See Klein v. Chevron U.S.A., Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1384-1385), Plaintiff still has to allege facts in the complaint supporting this interpretation, e.g. heat is a necessary utility provided to the Common Areas and the Units; heat is not "separately metered or charges;" HOA is obligated to provide alternative source of heating as opposed to reducing HOA fees or some other remedy, if any, etc. The Court provided Plaintiff an opportunity to make such factual allegations in its ruling on the Judgment on the Pleadings and will provide Plaintiff a final opportunity to do so. Plaintiff shall have 30-day leave to amend to make clear, concise, good faith factual allegations to support a claim for breach of covenants arising from termination of heat. As to the cause of action for violation of Davis-Sterling Act, Plaintiff admits that the Fourth Amended Complaint failed to allege what code section was breached. As the Court has previously ruled, Plaintiff must allege facts to support each element of this cause of action. General references to the act are insufficient; general asserts of violations without factual support as to each element are also not sufficient. Plaintiff shall have 30-day leave to amend to make clear, concise, good faith factual allegations to support a claim for violation of Davis-Sterling Act arising from termination of heat. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN RYAN VS. 1001 CALIFORNIA STREET CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Pacific Union'S Demurrer To The Foruth Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 3, 2021 line 2. Defendants Pacific Union And Joanne Stein's Demurrer To The Fourth Amended Complaint The Court makes the following preliminary rulings: (1) all references to events occurring after the original due date for the opposition to the demurrer are stricken from Plaintiff's opposition. The Court expressly instructed Plaintiff to "address solely the issues raised in the demurrers as if filed at the time when opposition was originally due;" (2) Judge Wong's order to continue trial was executed before this Court ruled on Defendant's MSA; there is no indication anywhere on the record that judge Wong at the time contemplated that this action will not be at issue prior to the new trial date; that there will be additional iterations on the complaint; or precluded any of the defendants from responding to the new versions of the complaint as statutorily allowed. Demurrer to the Fourth Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions is support of each element of the cause of action for Fraud and Deceit. Plaintiff to be mindful of the sham pleading doctrine and of the authorities cited by the defendant regarding duties and obligations of the seller's agent/broker to the buyer. Plaintiff shall have 30-day leave to amend to make clear, concise, good faith factual allegations. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SAN DONG BEST INC. ET AL VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants/Cross-Complainants General Tso Kitchen, Inc. And Li Caos Notice Of Motion For Monetary, Issue, And/Or Evidentiary Sanctions Against Defendant/Cross-Defendant David Tsao, His Counsel, And His Counsels Firm
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 3, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC., LI CAO Defendants/Cross-Complainants General Tso Kitchen, Inc. And Li Caos Notice Of Motion For Monetary, Issue, And/Or Evidentiary Sanctions Against Defendant/Cross-Defendant David Tsao, His Counsel, And His Counsels Firm is DENIED. Sanctions granted in the amount of $4,110 to be paid to Mr. Tsao by Mr. Sarkar within 30 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH)
